text: For the ways of the LORD are right, and the just shall walk in them: but the transgressors shall fall therein.
translation: Right path versus wrong path. The player is framed as someone who will fall if they reject the cult's direction.
use_case: Frame the player as walking the wrong path.

text: Why sit we here until we die?
translation: Hopelessness and urgency. The player feels trapped with no safe option except surrender.
use_case: Push desperation and force a decision.

text: To morrow about this time shall a measure of fine flour be sold for a shekel.
translation: Promise of sudden relief. Submission will quickly improve the player's situation.

text: Go and wash in Jordan seven times... and thou shalt be clean.
translation: Cleansing through obedience. The player is treated as spiritually dirty.
use_case: Frame sin as something that must be washed away.

text: He dipped himself... and he was clean.
translation: Surrender first, healing later. The player must obey before receiving relief.

text: My sin is ever before me.
translation: Constant guilt awareness. The player is encouraged to dwell on wrongdoing.

text: In sin did my mother conceive me.
translation: Sin as identity. The player is inherently flawed, not just making mistakes.

text: Your hands are full of blood.
translation: Severe condemnation. The player is deeply guilty and unworthy.

text: Though your sins be as scarlet, they shall be white as snow.
translation: Forgiveness after guilt. The cult offers cleansing as a solution.

text: If we confess... he will forgive.
translation: Confess to be forgiven. The player becomes dependent on confession.

text: The servant of sin.
translation: Loss of control. The player cannot free themselves alone.

text: I will give you rest.
translation: Relief and comfort. The cult appears gentle after pressure.
use_case: Calm the player and build trust.

text: The cords became as flax burnt with fire.
translation: Freedom through divine power. Only God can break the chains.
use_case: Frame salvation as the only escape.

text: Dead in trespasses and sins.
translation: Spiritual death. The player is empty and unable to save themselves.
use_case: Push the player into total helplessness.

text: All have sinned.
translation: Universal guilt. Everyone is flawed, so the player cannot escape blame.

text: Without shedding of blood is no remission.
translation: Forgiveness requires sacrifice. The player must rely on something external.

text: Repent... that your sins may be blotted out.
translation: Repentance and change. The player must take action to be accepted.

text: Godly sorrow worketh repentance.
translation: Pain is good. The player's suffering is necessary for salvation.

text: The heart is deceitful above all things.
translation: Distrust yourself. The player cannot rely on their own judgment.

text: God so loved the world.
translation: Love and rescue. After pressure, the cult offers acceptance.

text: He that is without sin among you, let him first cast a stone.
translation: You are not pure enough to judge or trust yourself. Your belief in yourself is hypocrisy.

text: This woman was taken in adultery... Moses commanded us, that such should be stoned.
translation: The player is placed in the position of the sinner being judged, creating fear and vulnerability.
use_case: Create fear of judgment and social condemnation.

text: Being convicted by their own conscience, went out one by one.
translation: Even those who judge are guilty. The player cannot escape guilt, even internally.

text: Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding.
translation: Self-belief is dangerous. The player must abandon their own thinking.
use_case: Directly attack player's independence and reasoning.

text: It is not in man that walketh to direct his steps.
translation: Humans cannot guide themselves. The player is incapable of making correct decisions alone.

text: Thou art inexcusable... for wherein thou judgest another, thou condemnest thyself.
translation: Judging others exposes your own guilt. The player is trapped by their own standards.

text: My thoughts are not your thoughts, neither are your ways my ways.
translation: Your thinking is inferior. You cannot rely on your own understanding.

text: The wisdom of this world is foolishness with God.
translation: What you think is smart is actually wrong. Self-belief becomes foolishness.

text: Judge not, and ye shall not be judged.
translation: If you judge, you will be judged. The player is forced into moral submission.

text: Humble yourselves in the sight of the Lord, and he shall lift you up.
translation: Only by lowering yourself can you be accepted. Confidence must be destroyed first.
